title: Oxygenator without integrated heat exchanger, with common heat exchanger for patient's systemic blood circulation and cardioplegia delivery system and methods thereof
abstract: A medical device for oxygenation, removal of carbon dioxide, temperature exchange in the patient's blood and, in addition, for delivering the cold/warm blood/crystalloid cardioplegia to the cardiac muscle in an extracorporeal circuit during heart surgery. A single heat exchanger is used either for patient's systemic blood circulation or cardioplegia delivery to the heart, whenever it is needed.
claims:
Claims im: An oxygenator (25) having an elliptic cylindrical housing (26); atleast one bundle of microporous capillary membrane fibers (44) disposed at an angle to the longitudinal axis of the said housing (26) within the said housing (26); and a filter (45) between the said fibers (44) and the said housing (26); characterised in that, the said housing (26) has an upper cap portion (32) with gas inlet port (50) , the upper cap portion (32) attached by a first mating feature (36) to a first potting means (34) to hold one end of the said bundle of fibers (44); a lower cap portion (33) having a gas outlet port (51) attached by second mating feature (37) to the second potting means (35) to hold the other end of the said bundle of fibers (44), the gas passes through each of the fibers (44) from one end and exits on the other end; an inlet (48) to receive blood and a first outlet (49) and second outlet (53) to exit oxygenated blood; and an outlet purge port (52) to remove air from the blood. The oxygenator (25) as claimed in claim 1 wherein the said housing (26) may further comprise a vent port between the filter (45) and the said fibers (44) of the body portion of housing (26) to flush out the debris from the blood. 

The oxygenator (25) as claimed in any of the preceding claims wherein, the said outlet port (49) of the oxygenator (25) further comprises of two more ports (54,55) to sample the oxygenated blood and monitor temperature of the oxygenated blood. The oxygenator (25) as claimed in any of the preceding claims wherein, the said fibers (44) are arranged preferably at an angle of 5Â° to 10Â° to central longitudinal axis of the said housing (26) . The oxygenator (25) as claimed in any of the preceding claims wherein, the said gas is substantially oxygen. An integrated perfusion device characterized in that the device comprises of: an oxygenator (25) having an elliptic cylindrical housing (26); atleast one bundle of microporous capillary membrane fibers (44) disposed at an angle to the longitudinal axis of the said housing (26) within the said housing (26); and a filter (45) between the said fibers (44) and the said housing (26); characterised in that, the said housing (26) has an upper cap portion (32) with gas inlet port (50), the upper cap portion (32) attached by a first mating feature (36) to a first potting means (34) to hold one end of the said bundle of 

fibers (44); a lower cap portion (33) having a gas outlet port (51) attached by second mating feature (37) to the second potting means (35) to hold the other end of the said bundle of fibers (44), the gas passes through each of the fibers (44) from one end and exits on the other end; an inlet (48) to receive blood and a first outlet (49) and second outlet (53) to exit oxygenated blood; and an outlet purge port (52) to remove air from the blood; and a heat exchanger (75) adapted to be attached to the first inlet (48) or the second outlet (53) of the oxygenator (25) , the heat exchanger (75) comprises of an inner cylindrical structure (79) surrounded by a middle cylindrical structure (78) having helical shaped outer surface (80), the inner cylindrical structure (79) attached to the middle cylindrical structure (78) by a circumferential array of pins (91a, 91b) thereby defining an inner flow passage between the inner (79) and middle (78) cylindrical structures, the passage having an inlet (86) and outlet (87) to receive and exit a first fluid; the middle cylindrical structure (78) enclosed by an outer cylindrical structure (77) defining an outer flow passage between the outer (77) and middle (78) cylindrical structures, the outer structure (77) having a first set of inlet (82) and outlet (83) to receive and exit blood; a second set of inlet (84) and outlet (85) to receive and exit a second fluid; and a purge port (81) to remove air from the blood or second fluid. 

The device as claimed in claim 6 wherein the said housing (26) may further comprise a vent port between the filter (45) and the said fibers (44) of the body portion of housing (26) to flush out the debris from the blood. The device as claimed in claims 6 or 7 wherein, the said outlet fluid port (49) of the oxygenator (25) further comprises of two more ports (54,55) to sample the oxygenated blood and monitor temperature of the oxygenated blood. The device as claimed in claims 6, 7 or 8 wherein, the said fibers (44) are arranged preferably at an angle of 5Â° to 10Â° to central longitudinal axis of the housing (26) . The device as claimed in claims 6, 7, 8 or 9 wherein, the first fluid is generally water. The device as claimed in claims 6, 7, 8, 9 or 10 wherein, the second fluid is selected from oxygenated blood, crystalloid cardioplegia fluid, or a combination of oxygenated blood and cardioplegia fluid. 12. The device as claimed in claims 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 or 11 wherein, the said gas is substantially oxygen. The device as claimed in claims 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 or 12 wherein, the first fluid and blood or the second fluid flow in the opposite direction to each other to maximize the heat exchange. The device as claimed in claims 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12 or 13 wherein the ratio of volume of heat exchanging fluid medium to volume of blood medium or cardioplegia medium is 2:1 at any given time. The device as claimed in claims 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13 or 14 wherein, the function of the single heat exchanger (75) is utilized for the second fluid in addition to the blood. A method of oxygenating a first fluid comprising the steps of: providing an oxygenator (25) having an elliptic cylindrical housing (26); atleast one bundle of microporous capillary membrane fibers (44) disposed at an angle to the longitudinal axis of the said housing (26) within the said housing (26); and a filter (45) between the said fibers (44) and the said housing (26); characterised in 

 that, the said housing (26) has an upper cap portion (32) with gas inlet port (50), the upper cap portion (32) attached by a first mating feature (36) to a first potting means (34) to hold one end of the said bundle of fibers (44); a lower cap portion (33) having a gas outlet port (51) attached by second mating feature (37) to the second potting means (35) to hold the other end of the said bundle of fibers (44), the gas passes through each of the fibers (44) from one end and exits on the other end; an inlet (48) to receive blood and a first outlet (49) and second outlet (53) to exit oxygenated blood; and an outlet purge port (52) to remove air from the blood; and circulating the blood through the said oxygenator (25) . 17. A method of performing a perfusion on a patient comprising the steps of: providing oxygenator (25) having an elliptic cylindrical housing (26); atleast one bundle of microporous capillary membrane fibers (44) disposed at an angle to the longitudinal axis of the said housing (26) within the said housing (26); and a filter (45) between the said fibers (44) and the said housing (26); characterised in that, the said housing (26) has an upper cap portion (32) with gas inlet port (50), the upper cap portion (32) attached by a first mating feature (36) to a first potting 

means (34) to hold one end of the said bundle of fibers (44); a lower cap portion (33) having a gas outlet port (51) attached by second mating feature (37) to the second potting means (35) to hold the other end of the said bundle of fibers (44), the gas passes through each of the fibers (44) from one end and exits on the other end; an inlet (48) to receive blood and a first outlet (49) and second outlet (53) to exit oxygenated blood; and an outlet purge port (52) to remove air from the blood; providing a heat exchanger (75) to transfer heat to a plurality of fluids, the heat exchanger (75) comprises of an inner cylindrical structure (79) surrounded by a middle cylindrical structure (78) having helical shaped outer surface (80), the inner cylindrical structure (79) attached to the middle cylindrical structure (78) by a circumferential array of pins (91a, 91b) thereby defining an inner flow passage between the inner (79) and middle (78) cylindrical structures, the passage having an inlet (86) and outlet (87) to receive and exit a first fluid; the middle cylindrical structure (78) enclosed by an outer cylindrical structure (77) defining an outer flow passage between the outer (77) and middle (78) cylindrical structures, the outer structure (77) having a first set of inlet (82) and outlet (83) to receive and exit blood; a second set of inlet (84) and outlet (85) to receive and exit a second fluid; and a purge port (81) to remove air from the blood or second fluid; 

 attaching the first inlet (48) or the second outlet (53) of the oxygenator (25) to the heat exchanger (75); and circulating the desired fluid through the heat exchanger (75) of the perfusion device.
